text: please dial 201-612-7415. Playback reservation number is 137-59-551. I would now let's turn

text: please go ahead.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you and good afternoon, everyone. Today's call will include prepared remarks by Cristiano
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Amón and Akash Palkwala. In addition, Alex Rogers will join the question and answer session.
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: You can access our earnings release and a slide presentation that will accompany this
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: call on our Investor Relations website. In addition, this call is being webcast on Qualcomm.com
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: and a replay will be available on our website later today. During the call today, we will
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: use non-gap financial measures as defined in Regulation G and you can find the related
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: reconciliations to gap on our website. We will also make forward-looking statements, including
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: projections and estimates of future events, business or industry trends, or business
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: or financial results. Actual events or results could differ materially from those projected
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: in our forward-looking statements. Please refer to our SEC filings, including our most
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: recent 10Q, which contain important factors that could cause actual results to differ
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: materially from the forward-looking statements. And now to comments from Qualcomm's President
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: and Chief Executive Officer Cristiano Amón.
speaker: BRETT SIMPSON - SVP, INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thank you, Brett. And good afternoon, everyone. Thanks for joining us today. In fiscal Q2,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: we delivered revenues of $10.6 billion in non-gap earnings per share of $2.65 with
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: EPS coming in at the high end of our guidance. QCT revenues were $9.1 billion with another
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: quarter of record automotive revenues as well as growth in IoT. Licensing business revenues
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: were $1.4 billion. Before I share key highlights from the business, I would like to provide
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: some perspective on Qualcomm's current customer design cycles and the opportunities ahead.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: We are in a period of profound change, and it may not yet seem obvious to the financial
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: community. The emergence of agent AI workloads with Open Claw as an early example are fundamentally
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: changing user experiences across connected edge devices and reshaping our roadmap in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: every platform we develop. For agents to work efficiently, they must run continuously in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the background, few sensor data into context, orchestrate multi-step tasks reliably and deliver
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: strong security. Today's install base of devices were not built for this new capabilities,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and it represents a significant upgrade opportunity and expansion of our addressable market in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the coming years. Agent orchestration is predominantly CPU bound, and Qualcomm has the words best
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: performing CPU across smartphones, PCs, IoT, and soon the data center. Qualcomm's unparalleled
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: connectivity solutions and power efficient NPU for local models will also be key assets to
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: delivering agent AI experiences. No other semiconductor company matches the breadth and scale of our
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: technology and product portfolio, which powers devices spanning milliwatts to kilowatts, from
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: smart wearables to data centers. As a result, we're seeing a step function increase in strategic
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: customer engagement and is changing how we think about the broad AI opportunity as well as the
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: speed of our diversification efforts. Beginning with automotive, in Q2, we exceeded $5 billion in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: annualized revenues for the first time, and we expect to exit Cisco 26 at a run rate above $6
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: billion. This growth is driven by our fourth generation Snapdragon Digital Chassis platform,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: which comprises connectivity, telematics, infotainment, as well as advanced driver assistant and
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: automated driving. Notably, we have now enabled more than one million cars operating A-Dassen
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: economy on our Snapdragon ride processors. By the end of the fiscal year, we will begin
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: commercial shipments of our fifth generation Snapdragon Digital Chassis platform. This represents
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the largest generation-to-generation content increase in Qualcomm's history, delivering three
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: times higher CPU throughput, a three-fold increase in GPU capability and 12 times higher NPU
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: performance while supporting in-vehicle agents and processing for level three and level four
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: autonomous driving. Looking ahead to fiscal 27, we expect continued share gains and increased
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: content, particularly in A-Dass, where pleased with the performance of our automated driving
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: stack of BMW, and we're seeing broad customer engagement from other leading automakers.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Our recent announcements with Bosch and Wave are good examples of what's to come as we build on
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: our proven platforms and self-driving stack in scale A-Dass. In IoT,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Agentic workloads and Adjai are driving major product renewal design cycles. Overall, our pipeline
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: is healthy and there is clear momentum for Qualcomm's solutions. In personal AI, we expect a significant
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: increase in the choice of newest more glasses starting in the second half of the year. We believe
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: these launches combined with a rapid progress in Agentic AI will catalyze an inflection point
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: in customer demand across this category. Our 2026 Snapdragon X2PC platforms are currently in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: production and our world-class Orion CPU unlocks powerful, always-on, Agentic experiences,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: making it a true competitive differentiator. Agentic orchestrators, such as Open Claw,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex Desktop, perplexity computer,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: crew AI, air-miss agent, land graph, and humane one running on Snapdragon X2 or early proof points.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: A recent PCMag review of the ASUS ZenBook A16 notes the Qualcomm is now a serious challenger
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: in the PC space and states, quote, the generational leap from the original Snapdragon X Elite to
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the X2 series is particularly striking. Qualcomm hasn't just caught up to the industry. In some cases,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: is now helping to set the pace, unquote. In addition, our Exagon NPU is the world's fastest
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: four laptops delivering up to 85 tops together with our industry leading CPU, which has the best
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: on-device token generation rate. Snapdragon X2 delivers the full agent experience
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: end-to-end and outperforms Intel's Penter Lake by nearly 30%. In physical and industrial AI,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: our new Dragon Wing IQ10 platform has generated substantial customer interest since our launch
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: at CES. This is a significant upgrade compared to IQ9, feature an NPU with up to 700 tops of own
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: device AI performance, an 18-core Orion CPU over 20 camera sensors in an integrated safety island.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Building on our design win with Figure AI, we announced an exciting multi-year agreement with
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: NURA, reinforcing our confidence that we can become a significant player in the broad robotics market.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Also during the quarter, we introduced Ventuno Q at Embedded World. This is the second Arduino
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: platform built on Qualcomm Silicon, and we view it as a word-class prototyping engine for both
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: robotics and industrial AI developers as we expand our ecosystem across K verticals. Ventuno Q
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: is purpose-built to bring AI into the physical world, enabling fully autonomous AI agents in
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: a wide range of edge AI applications, including voice assistance and vision systems. Several new
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: industrial AI products are also moving from design win to deployment across retail, utilities,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: oil and gas, agriculture and other verticals. In data center, the AlphaWave integration is off
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: to a great start, and we're pursuing multiple opportunities with large hyperscalers, cloud service
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: providers, Southern AI projects and other global partners. Building on that momentum,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: we're also entering the custom silicon space, beginning our ramp with a leading hyperscaler,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and we expect initial shipments in the December quarter. In addition, development of our leading
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: data center CPU and high performance AI inference accelerators is progressing well. We look forward
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: to sharing more details and customer wins at Investor Day in June. Regarding handsets,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: I would like to underscore two key points. First, the quarter played out as we expected.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Cell-true held up in our chip business materially under shipped consumer demand.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: We believe our China Android revenue is bottoming out in fiscal Q3 and Akash will provide more
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: specifics in his financial update. Second, we think agent smart phones will soon begin to
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: influence the pre-menteer, and we expect this team will only get stronger into fiscal 27.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: With examples like the Biden's Dow Bao-powered agent AI phone from ZTE Nubia.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Xiaomi's recent announcement of a Mi-Claw agent framework and other agent systems now in development
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: across the Android ecosystem. We have a clear line of sight into how the AI upgrade cycle will
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: unfold, and this is going to be an important day wind for premium demand over time.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Next, I want to highlight a major strategic initiative and long-term growth driver for Qualcomm.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: 6G, the next generation of wireless. Design for the age of AI will believe 6G will present
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: one of the most significant transitions for the wireless industry. From a connectivity perspective,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: 6G will enable new classes of mobile and personal devices, such as smart glasses,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: with enhanced uplink capabilities to support agent use cases like C, what I see.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Beyond connectivity, 6G will be an AI native network where AI reasoning, learning, and autonomous
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: action are core functions. It is intended to act as distributed intelligent infrastructure
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: that integrates communication in wide area real-time sensing. With this new capabilities,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the network becomes critical infrastructure and provides the telecom industry an opportunity
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: to develop completely new business and economic models. It will make possible new AI-enabled
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: services ranging from context-relevant data, data insights, and analytics, low altitude
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: are aerial, terrestrial, and autonomous traffic management, drone detection and tracking,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and 3D mapping with telemetry to build dynamic digital twins at scale.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Qualcomm's leadership in connectivity, AI processing, and high-performance low-power computing
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: position us to be one of the key architects and beneficiaries of the 6G transition.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: In addition to the development of foundational technologies and standards,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: we're building end-to-end solutions for devices and the network from agentic modems and compute
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: platforms that power phones, PC, intelligent wearables, and cars all the way to the network,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: including power-efficient, next-generation radar units, wide-area network sensing platforms,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and high-performance compute and AI accelerators for the RAN, network edge, core, and data center.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: To help shape and accelerate the 6G roadmap, at MWC, we launch a 60-company coalition
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: spanning carriers, cloud infrastructure, AI-native partners, and auto OEMs.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: The engagement and feedback on our 6G vision and plans from our partners, customers, and
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: governments across the globe has been very positive, and we look forward to working across the industry
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: to deliver on this generational opportunity. Before I turn the call over to Akash, I want to note
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: that we will provide a broader update at our investor day to include our data center plans
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and our progress in other areas, including advanced robotics, next-generation ADAS,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: industrial edge AI, personal AI devices, and 6G. We hope you can join us as we will be highlighting
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: meaningful new avenues of growth to support our long-term diversification story. I will now turn
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the call to Akash. Thank you, Kishiano, and good afternoon, everyone. Let me begin with our
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: results for the second fiscal quarter. We delivered revenues of $10.6 billion and non-gap EPS of
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: $2.65 with EPS at the high end of our guidance. QTL revenues of $1.4 billion and EBT margin of 72%
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: came in at the high end of our guidance, driven by favorable mix with global handset units
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: approximately flat on a year-over-year basis. QCT revenues of $9.1 billion and EBT margin of 27%
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: were in line with our expectations. QCT handset revenues of $6 billion came in as anticipated,
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: as OEMs remained cautious on handset bills due to the impact of challenging memory industry dynamics.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: QCT IoT revenues of $1.7 billion were up 9% on a year-over-year basis, driven by growth across
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: consumer and industrial products. In QCT automotive, we delivered another record quarter with revenues
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: of $1.3 billion representing 38% year-over-year growth, driven by accelerating demand and increasing
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: content per vehicle due to the transition of new digital cockpit and 8S launches to our fourth
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: generation chipsets. On a combined basis, QCT automotive and IOT revenues grew 20% year-over-year
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: underscoring the continued diversification of our business consistent with our long-term revenue
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: targets. We also returned $3.7 billion to stockholders during the quarter, including $2.8 billion in
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: cherry purchases and $945 million in dividends, reflecting acceleration of our capital return
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: program. Lastly, we released a previously recorded tax valuation allowance resulting in a $5.7 billion
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: non-cash gap tax benefit in the second fiscal quarter. This benefit is excluded from non-gap
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: results. This reversal reflects new guidance on corporate alternative minimum tax, issued in February
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: by Treasury and IRS permitting taxpayers to deduct previously capitalized domestic R&D expenses.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: Before turning to guidance, I'd like to provide an update on the continued impact of memory
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: industry dynamics on our business. Last quarter, we highlighted that the increasing demand for
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: memory in AI data centers was driving uncertainty in memory supply and price increases to hands-out
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: OEMs. As a result, the hands-out OEMs, particularly in China, were taking a cautious approach by
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: reducing build plans and drawing down channel inventory. These dynamics played out as expected
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: in the second fiscal quarter and are also reflected in our third quarter guidance.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: As a result, in both quarters, our China QCT Android shipments are meaningfully below the scale of
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: end consumer hands-out demand. We now estimate that QCT hands-out revenues from Chinese customers
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: will reach a bottom in the third quarter and return to sequential growth in the following quarter.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: Now, turning to guidance. In the third fiscal quarter, we are forecasting revenues of $9.2 to
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: $10 billion and non-gap EPS of $2.10 to $2.30. In QTL, we estimate revenues of 1.15 to $1.35
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: billion and EBT margins of 67 to 71 percent, with sequential decline primarily due to the operating
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: assumption of weaker low-tier hands-out units. In QCT, we expect revenues of 7.9 to 8.5 billion
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: and EBT margins of 25 to 27 percent. We are forecasting QCT hands-out revenues to be approximately
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: $4.9 billion as a result of the impact of the industry-wide memory dynamics I just outlined.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: We anticipate QCT IoT revenues to grow by high single digits versus the year ago period
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: driven by industrial and consumer products. In QCT automotive, following another record quarter,
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: we expect year over year revenue growth to further accelerate to approximately 50 percent
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: in the third fiscal quarter. Lastly, we forecast non-gap operating expenses to be approximately
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: $2.6 billion in the quarter. In closing, while our near-term revenues are impacted by memory
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: industry cyclical dynamics, we are confident in the underlying fundamentals around Snapdragon
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: product leadership and content growth opportunities, including the adoption of Agentec AI technologies.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: We continue to execute on our secular growth opportunities in automotive
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: minority and remain confident in achieving our long-term revenue targets. In addition,
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: we are very excited about the progress in our data center products and customer traction.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: We now expect initial shipments for a custom silicon engagement at a leading hyperscaler
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: later this calendar year. We look forward to providing an update on our growth initiatives,
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: including opportunities in data center and physical AI at our investor day on June 24th.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: This concludes our prepared remarks. Back to you, Brad.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: Thank you, Akash. All prior, we are now ready for questions.

text: Please just see your three questions. Hey, guys. Thank you for taking my question.

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: you, which is alongside what we said in the script. I think we have spent time, I think,

text: building assets in the building or CPU. We have a different solution for memory in the

text: accelerator. We have added a lot of capabilities for custom ASIC with the acquisition of AlphaWave

text: and connectivity we have been pursuing. Custom ASIC, we talk about having engagement with a

text: number of companies and please with the engagement several quarters ago. I think giving

text: the capabilities that we're developing and what's happening in the market, that's accelerating.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: We're very excited. The only thing I can tell, it is a large hyperscaler. We're really thinking
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: about a multi-generation engagement, but I think that's what we can say at this point.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: I guess we'll stay tuned. To my follow-up, can you maybe walk through why you're confident that,
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: I assume it's fiscal third quarter that you're referring to with the third quarter, but why
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: are confident fiscal third quarter can be the bottom for Android sales, Android QCT sales into
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: China. September quarter is usually a typically down seasonal quarter and just given how low
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: visibility is right now overall in the handset market, I'd be curious just what inputs you're
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: seeing that gives you the confidence it's going to bottom in the June quarter. Thank you.
speaker: JOSHUA BUCHALTER - TD COWEN

text: Sure, Josh. It's a cash. If you think about the impact to QCT
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: from Chinese handset OEMs as a result of the memory dynamics, it's really two parts. The first
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: part is the scale of the handset market. There we've seen some small decline in it, especially in
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: the mid-low tiers. By far, the larger impact have been the OEMs making a decision to slow down
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: their bills and draw down on channel inventory. Both of these factors have been in our March
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: quarter results and they're also represented in our June quarter guidance.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: And so what we end up doing in both quarters is really significantly undershipping the end
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: consumer demand for handsets as a result of the channel inventory drawn-on factor.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: So as we look forward, we feel confident that the third quarter is now the bottom. And so as we
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: go forward, the revenue is going to be much closer to the scale of the handset business versus
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: the inventory drawn-on factor continuing into our forecast.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: And Josh, this is Christian. I just want to add one thing because
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: there's another way to look into this. As you know, because of our licensing business,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: we do have visibility of what happens in the market. So we know how the
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: sell-through market is behaving even when increased price on the handsets. So it gives us a real good
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: idea on activations and customer demand versus what we're shipping. So that dynamic
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Great, great question. Look, I'll give it our perspective and I think we have now,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: I think more clarity than we ever have about where kind of we are in the AI space. So
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: a little bit of maybe at a very high level, right, in the beginning it was all about training.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: It was all about creation of AI, a lot of GPU, very GPU-centered deployment.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: Infr
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: and then the conversation changed to I'm going to use my GPU from training on the cluster that I
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: build and when I'm not training, I'm going to use that for inference. As inference starts to
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: game scale, you started to see dedicated solutions. The data center becomes more
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: disaggregated. You have separate computing solution, some for compute bounds, some for memory
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: bounds and now we're entering the, I'll say the next phase, which how AI is really going from
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: inference generating tokens. How do you generate demand for tokens, which all those
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: agentic experience and those orchestrators that run into a lot of the devices.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: So when you think of that landscape and you look at our IP and the places that we can be
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: very differentiated, I will start by our CPU. I think when you think about agents,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: CPU becomes very important and I will argue we were one of the companies that have a pretty good
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: CPU asset. We proven that CPU performance, we have leading performance on the markets that we
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: are right now such as PC, smartphone and auto and we have built and we'll provide details on
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: investor day, a dedicated CPU for agentic experiences in the data center. We're going to show the
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: metrics, we're going to show how perform people will be able to compare. As you know, we have an
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: architecture license and we have a very, very high performance CPU. So that's one of the assets.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: The other asset is how you think about the scale of a semiconductor company like Qualcomm. We're
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: not small and the ability to combine the IP with the ability to do custom silicon, make sure that
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: that yield, make sure it is delivered with quality and combine a lot of the connectivity IP
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: which I believe AlphaWave because it was a licensing IP company has a leading IP. The more you license,
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the better your IP becomes. The number three is how we think about the accelerator. You're going to
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: need high compute density low TCO and we think that we have something unique which is
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: focused on a cluster that is disaggregating for very specific function especially like
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: decode. I think the activity you've seen with companies like Grok and CibriBros just prove
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: that you have opportunity for a dedicated inference accelerator. And the last point is I will not discount
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: the position that we have on the edge. If you actually track what's happening with open claw
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: in all of the different desktop and co-work solutions, you rely a lot on a high performance
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: CPU device which is also causing an upgrade cycle for us. So we look at this whole landscape and
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: that's how we feel so good about the agentic transition of AI, what it means to Qualcomm.
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: And hopefully on June 24 we'll show the details on the roadmap and if after we'll be able to see
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: where we stand. And please reserve a seat. Yes, no, please look forward to talking about that
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: more at the investor today. Maybe for my follow up just going back to the handset business. Can

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: more and more development of L2++ and direction towards level three, you're starting to see the
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: amount of computing power going up. So for us, it's basically a significant revenue accelerator
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: within automotive. Specifically on your question on gross margin, the highlight may be two
speaker: CRISTIANO AMON - PRESIDENT & CEO

text: additional factors to what Cristiano said. I think we have the transitioning from a chip sale to a
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: SIP sale, and so as we go to a module, it increases the revenue opportunity for us as well. And then
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: in addition, we have software opportunity on top of the chip set, which also helps our margin profile.
speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

speaker: AKASH PALKHIWALA - EVP, CFO & COO

text: business that has several vectors of growth as both of us are aligned.
